response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack web and platform engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the overlap with this role is minimal. The most disqualifying gap is the absence of Japanese language proficiency, listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his background is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and web infrastructure — not embedded C development, Linux kernel internals, or network security protocols. He has a brief stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments and some exposure to Linux, but nothing approaching the embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-feature domain this role demands.
strengths: Long career with demonstrated breadth across systems, embedded-adjacent work (robotic arms, hardware at Altom, NI LabWindows C/C++), and low-latency systems (Sociomantic DLang microservices)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, code quality culture, and open-source contributions show professional maturity
Comfortable with Linux and REST APIs, providing a baseline that could be built upon in a different role
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated non-negotiable hard requirement for the position
No experience with any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange) or embedded networking (L2/L3, SNMP, NETCONF, switch chips)
C programming is only a marginal part of his history (one role, 6 months); the bulk of his career is TypeScript/Node.js/web, which is largely irrelevant to embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
